summary: The case shows post-concussive cognitive vulnerability (sustained attention), sensory provocation (screen-nausea), emotional reactivity (irritability), and maladaptive beliefs about permanence and causality after a concussion months prior, with measurable daily-life impact. A multi-domain, modifiable-lever strategy is proposed across bio, psycho, and social factors (and their interactions) to reduce objective concentration lapses, decrease sensory-provoked symptoms, improve emotion regulation, recalibrate permanent-belief assumptions, and restore functional participation, using measurable, actionable predictors rather than symptom labels alone.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured attention training and cognitive remediation program (targeted computerized tasks plus real-world strategy coaching) to improve sustained attention and reduce task-related lapses.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Direct development of executive attention networks and compensatory strategies reduces sustained attention lapses, with transfer to work/study and screen-based tasks.
solutions.measurement: Pre/post objective attention metrics (e.g., continuous performance task indices), task completion rates, and self-reported concentration during typical activities.

solutions.solution_candidate: Environmental adjustments and graded screen exposure plan (brightness filters, blue-light reduction, breaks, and a stepwise reintroduction protocol).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing sensory load and gradually re-training tolerance lowers nausea and allows for more stable concentration during screen-based tasks.
solutions.measurement: Nausea ratings during screen use (0-10), daily screen time, and task efficiency on screen-based work.
solutions.safety_notes: If nausea worsens or dizziness occurs, slow exposure and consult clinician; consider ophthalmology if persistent.

solutions.solution_candidate: Emotion regulation skills training (DBT-inspired and mindfulness-based techniques) to reduce irritability thresholds and improve frustration tolerance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced regulatory repertoire decreases emotional reactivity to cognitive load and stressors, reducing irritability in daily interactions.
solutions.measurement: Daily irritability ratings (0-10), frequency of irritable episodes, and persistence on challenging tasks.

solutions.solution_candidate: Psychoeducation and cognitive restructuring addressing permanence fears and predominant concussion attribution, with collaborative medical context.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reframing catastrophic beliefs and attributions fosters engagement with rehabilitation and reduces distress.
solutions.measurement: Belief about permanence scale; recovery expectations; treatment engagement indicators.
solutions.safety_notes: Careful to validate concerns and coordinate with medical follow-up; monitor for rising distress.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured accommodations for daily tasks (task breakdowns, calendars, checklists, and workload pacing) with workplace/school support.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizing task structure reduces cognitive load and reduces stress-related irritability, enabling better concentration in daily activities.
solutions.measurement: Functional impairment ratings; daily task completion; self-reported productivity at work/study/leisure.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for over-reliance on external aids; promote gradual independence.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep optimization plan (consistent schedule, pre-bed wind-down, limiting screens before bed, daytime light exposure) to support brain recovery.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep enhances attention, reduces sensory sensitivity, and supports emotional regulation; better sleep may dampen irritability and improve task performance.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality (sleep logs/actigraphy), daytime alertness, and self-reported screen tolerance.
solutions.safety_notes: If sleep disturbances persist, refer to sleep specialist.

solutions.solution_candidate: Use of external aids (calendars, reminders, checklists, voice notes) and training in offloading strategies to minimize cognitive load.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Externalizing cognitive demands reduces mental load and supports sustained attention and task completion in everyday life.
solutions.measurement: Daily task success rate; self-reported concentration during activities; usage analytics of digital aids.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure privacy and data security for digital tools.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured social engagement and participation plans (peer check-ins, meaningful activities) to reduce isolation and improve functional participation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social connectedness buffers stress, supports motivation, and improves adherence to rehabilitation and daily functioning.
solutions.measurement: Social connectedness scale; participation frequency; self-rated functional engagement.
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for social overload and ensure consent/ boundaries.
